question: How does Excalidraw render and update canvas elements?

graph.answer:
Excalidraw uses layered canvas rendering, not one DOM node per shape.

1. The `Scene` is the invalidation source. `replaceAllElements()` and `mutateElement()` both end in `triggerUpdate()`, which bumps `sceneNonce` and notifies listeners. See [Scene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/element/src/Scene.ts#L271), [Scene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/element/src/Scene.ts#L300), [Scene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/element/src/Scene.ts#L303), [Scene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/element/src/Scene.ts#L427), [Scene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/element/src/Scene.ts#L441).

2. `App` subscribes to those scene updates with `this.scene.onUpdate(this.triggerRender)`. On each render pass it calls `renderer.getRenderableElements(...)` to compute what should be painted, then passes that into three canvas layers. See [App.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L879), [App.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L2144), [App.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L3235), [App.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L4800).

3. The scene is split into three canvas layers:
- `StaticCanvas` draws the main scene layer: grid, visible elements, links, embeddables, and pending flowchart nodes via `renderStaticScene()`. See [StaticCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/StaticCanvas.tsx#L37), [StaticCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/StaticCanvas.tsx#L44), [staticScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/staticScene.ts#L272), [staticScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/staticScene.ts#L287), [staticScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/staticScene.ts#L488).
- `NewElementCanvas` draws only the currently in-progress `newElement`, and clears itself when there is no active shape to draw. See [NewElementCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/NewElementCanvas.tsx#L27), [renderNewElementScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/renderNewElementScene.ts#L70), [renderNewElementScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/renderNewElementScene.ts#L102).
- `InteractiveCanvas` draws transient UI: selection boxes, transform handles, snap lines, remote cursors, and scrollbars via `renderInteractiveScene()`. It is also animated through `AnimationController`. See [InteractiveCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/InteractiveCanvas.tsx#L90), [InteractiveCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/InteractiveCanvas.tsx#L174), [interactiveScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/interactiveScene.ts#L1591), [interactiveScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/interactiveScene.ts#L2031), [interactiveScene.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/renderer/interactiveScene.ts#L2035).

4. `Renderer.getRenderableElements()` is the main pre-pass that decides which scene elements belong on the static layer. It filters out the element currently being edited as text, keeps the active `newElement` off the static layer when needed, and can reorder dragged selected elements when they are being highlighted into a frame. It also derives `canvasNonce` from the scene nonce, plus the in-progress element nonce when relevant. See [Renderer.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/scene/Renderer.ts#L89), [Renderer.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/scene/Renderer.ts#L161), [Renderer.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/scene/Renderer.ts#L210), [Renderer.ts](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/scene/Renderer.ts#L235).

5. Redraws are mostly whole-layer redraws, but the React wrappers are memoized so they only rerun when the relevant props change. That is why `canvasNonce`, `visibleElements`, `elementsMap`, and key app-state fields are the real triggers, not every unrelated state update. See [StaticCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/StaticCanvas.tsx#L108), [InteractiveCanvas.tsx](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/excalidraw/packages/excalidraw/components/canvases/InteractiveCanvas.tsx#L275).

If you want, I can also map this into a single “pointer move -> scene mutate -> canvas redraw” sequence for a concrete action like dragging, resizing, or typing text.
